title: hypericum alpinum var. latifolium
intro: Hypericum alpinum var. latifolium, also known as St. John's Wort Alpine, is a plant species in the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its narrow, pointed leaves and yellow flowers that bloom in the summer months. Traditionally, Hypericum alpinum var. latifolium has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and depression, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hypericum alpinum var. latifolium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
